The expression \( T_{(ab)} \omega^{[ab]} \) involves contracting the symmetric part of \( T_{ab} \) with the antisymmetric part of \( \omega^{ab} \). The symmetric part is defined as \( T_{(ab)} = \frac{1}{2}(T_{ab} + T_{ba}) \), and the antisymmetric part as \( \omega^{[ab]} = \frac{1}{2}(\omega^{ab} - \omega^{ba}) \). Their contraction yields:

\[
T_{(ab)} \omega^{[ab]} = \frac{1}{4}(T_{ab} + T_{ba})(\omega^{ab} - \omega^{ba}) = \frac{1}{4}\left( T_{ab}\omega^{ab} - T_{ab}\omega^{ba} + T_{ba}\omega^{ab} - T_{ba}\omega^{ba} \right).
\]

Relabeling dummy indices shows that \( T_{ab}\omega^{ba} = T_{ba}\omega^{ab} \) and \( T_{ba}\omega^{ba} = T_{ab}\omega^{ab} \). Substituting these identities leads to cancellation of all terms, resulting in zero. More generally, the contraction of any symmetric tensor with an antisymmetric tensor vanishes.

\boxed{0}
